# sqlite-graphrag (v1.0.99 LLM-Only One-Shot)

> Persistent memory for 27 AI agents in a single 14.6 MiB Rust binary (LLM-only, one-shot)

27 AI agents. One 14.6 MiB binary. Zero model download. sqlite-graphrag v1.0.99 (current; v1.0.99 GAP-SG-67 removed destructive degree-cap pruning + `--max-entity-degree` flag from remember/link (BREAKING, clap exit 2; writes purely additive, never delete edges); GAP-SG-68 aligned `graph entities --sort-by degree` doc to ascending behaviour (`--order desc` for most-connected-first); GAP-SG-69 `enrich body-enrich --until-empty` converges (scan skips preservation-vetoed bodies); no migration, schema v15; v1.0.97 enrich dead-letter recovery + write ergonomics: `--requeue-dead`/`--list-dead`/`--ignore-backoff`/`--prune-dead-orphans` (deletes dead memory queue entries whose key no longer exists in the main db, GAP-SG-66, ADR-0058), `--status`/`--list-dead`/`--requeue-dead`/`--prune-dead-orphans` run without `--operation`/`--mode`, new `augment-bindings` operation (requires `--names`), `body-extract --body-extract-graph-only`, `--max-attempts` default 8, `--openrouter-timeout` default 600s; `remember --graph-file`/`--strict-name`/`--replace-graph`; `ingest --force-merge` with `body_hash` dedup and native large-body auto-split; `read --format raw`; `unlink --memory/--entity`; `embedding status` `coverage` object; `stats` top-level `total_memories`; `--db` belongs after the subcommand while `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_DB_PATH` is the canonical override (GAP-20, SG-32); v1.0.96 enrich dead-letter + bounded REST fan-out (ADR-0055): `enrich --until-empty` runs an internal scan->drain loop until the eligible queue empties or `--max-runtime` expires, a dead-letter queue adds `error_class`/`next_retry_at` columns plus a terminal `dead` status so the backlog strictly converges (GAP-ENRICH-BACKLOG-CONVERGE), new `--max-attempts`/`--status`/`--rest-concurrency` flags, and OpenRouter REST embedding fans out per 32-chunk batch via a bounded `tokio::task::JoinSet` (GAP-OPENROUTER-REST-CONCURRENCY); v1.0.95 OpenRouter chat enrich (ADR-0054, GAP-OR-ENRICH): `enrich --mode openrouter` routes the enrich judge to the OpenRouter `/chat/completions` REST endpoint with no local CLI; v1.0.94 four-gap remediation: default embedding dim 384, enrich --mode required, embedding timeout 300s, entity embedding honours the selected backend; v1.0.93 added optional OpenRouter REST API embedding backend via `--embedding-backend openrouter` with ~100-500ms latency vs 20-60s subprocess LLM, `--enrich-after` flag for ingest, 5 BUG-OR fixes; v1.0.91 fixed GAP-SPAWN-001 spawn CWD isolation, BUG-17 degree fix, BUG-15/16 schema fixes; v1.0.90 added OpenCode backend; v1.0.89 schema drift via schemars; v1.0.87 preflight validation layer; v1.0.79 was the LLM-only foundation) gives every AI coding assistant a local, fast, private memory layer backed by a single SQLite file. Embedding generation uses EITHER a headless LLM subprocess (claude/codex/opencode via OAuth) OR the OpenRouter REST API (v1.0.93, via `--embedding-backend openrouter`). No daemon, no ONNX runtime, no model download. Graph-native retrieval. Deterministic JSON output ready for pipeline orchestration.

- Read this document in [Portuguese (pt-BR)](llms.pt-BR.txt).
- Architecture rationale: [ADR-0019](docs/decisions/adr-0019-llm-only-one-shot.md) (LLM-only one-shot), [ADR-0020](docs/decisions/adr-0020-pure-rust-cosine.md) (pure-Rust cosine), [ADR-0021](docs/decisions/adr-0021-deprecate-daemon.md) (daemon deprecated), [ADR-0022](docs/decisions/adr-0022-blob-embeddings.md) (BLOB-backed embeddings), [ADR-0023](docs/decisions/adr-0023-remove-tokenizers.md) (tokenizers removed), [ADR-0024](docs/decisions/adr-0024-fts5-coarse-cosine-refine.md) (FTS5 coarse + cosine refine), [ADR-0025](docs/decisions/adr-0025-oauth-only-embedding.md) (OAuth-only LLM), [ADR-0052](docs/decisions/adr-0052-openrouter-embedding-backend.md) (OpenRouter embedding backend)


## v1.0.85 Architecture (LLM-Only, supersedes v1.0.79)
- The default build is LLM-only and one-shot. There is no daemon, no ONNX runtime, no `libonnxruntime.so` to ship, and no `multilingual-e5-small` model to download. The LLM subprocess IS the model.
- Embedding generation: a headless `claude code` (Anthropic OAuth) or `codex` (ChatGPT Pro OAuth) subprocess is spawned per call. The CLI never holds an embedding model in memory.
- Vector search: cosine similarity is computed in pure Rust over the BLOB embeddings stored in `memory_embeddings`, `entity_embeddings`, and `chunk_embeddings`. The `sqlite-vec` extension is gone.
- Daemon: deprecated in v1.0.76 and FULLY REMOVED in v1.0.79 (ahead of the v1.1.0 schedule). The CLI is 100% one-shot with zero IPC.
- `embedding-legacy`, `ner-legacy` and `full` features: REMOVED in v1.0.79 (ahead of the v1.1.0 schedule) together with the optional fastembed/ort/ndarray/tokenizers/hf-hub dependencies. Every build is LLM-only.
- Embedding pipeline (G42, v1.0.79): default dimensionality 64 (`--embedding-dim`, `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_EMBEDDING_DIM`, range [8, 4096]; precedence flag > env > `schema_meta.dim` > 384); batched calls (calibration bases of 8 chunks / 25 entity names at dim 64, adapted as clamp(baseĂ—64/dim, 1, base) â€” G44); bounded parallelism via `--llm-parallelism` on `remember` (default 4), `ingest` (default 2) and `edit`; every subprocess uses `kill_on_drop` plus `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_EMBED_TIMEOUT_SECS` (default 300s).
- Canonical re-embed: `enrich --operation re-embed --limit N --resume`; single-memory re-embed via `edit --force-reembed`.
- Migration from v1.0.74/v1.0.75: `sqlite-graphrag migrate --to-llm-only --drop-vec-tables --db <path>` is the one-shot upgrade. Combines checksum rewrite (--rehash) with V013 vec-table drop. The BLOB-backed embedding tables stay empty until the next `remember` / `edit` / `ingest` re-embeds via the LLM.


## v1.0.86 â†’ v1.0.91 Architecture (Pre-flight, Schema Drift, Flag Parity, OpenCode Backend, Spawn Isolation)
- v1.0.86 added 10 LLM-pipeline subcommands: `pending list|show|cleanup`, `embedding status|list|abandon`, `pending-embeddings list|process`, `slots status|release`. New global flags: `--max-concurrency`, `--wait-lock`, `--llm-parallelism`, `--ingest-parallelism`, `--graceful-shutdown-secs`, `--skip-embedding-on-failure`. Host-wide slot semaphore via `fs4` with `fcntl(F_SETLK)` on Unix and `LockFileEx` on Windows (ADR-0036, 0037, 0038, 0039, 0040)
- v1.0.87 added the pre-flight validation layer: `src/spawn/preflight.rs` (â‰Ą200 lines, 7 guards, 15 unit tests) gates every LLM subprocess spawn BEFORE the fork. New `AppError::PreFlightFailed(PreFlightError)` with `exit_code() == 16` (`EX_CONFIG`, `is_permanent() == true`). The 7 guards in order: `check_argv_size`, `check_binary_exists`, `check_mcp_config_inline`, `check_mcp_config_path`, `check_walkup_mcp_json`, `check_output_buffer`, `check_claude_config_dir`. Bypass via `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_SKIP_PREFLIGHT=1` (ADR-0045, GAP-META-005)
- v1.0.88 hotfixes: BUG-11 (CRITICAL) preflight failure in `extract/llm_embedding.rs:563-565` now propagates to `remember` via `embed_via_backend_strict`; BUG-12 (MEDIUM) OAuth-only enforcement emits 1 stderr line (was 2); BUG-13 (MEDIUM) `link --create-missing` respects entity-name validation. 11 new regression tests (ADR-0046, ADR-0047)
- v1.0.89 schema drift + flag parity: `health.schema.json` regenerated via `schemars` derive macro (Must-Ignore policy per RFC 7493 I-JSON, `additionalProperties: true`); 17 new fields added. 5 subcommands now accept `--db <PATH>`: `embedding status|list|abandon`, `pending list|show`. `migrate --dry-run --json` reports pending migrations without applying. `codex-models --json` accepted as no-op. `ingest --auto-describe` (default true) extracts description from first meaningful body line. `health --namespace <NS> --json` filters counts. Binary size 14.6 MiB documented in `Cargo.toml:6` (ADR-0048, ADR-0049)
- v1.0.90 OpenCode backend integration (ADR-0051): third LLM backend `opencode run` alongside `codex exec` and `claude -p`. New `--llm-backend opencode`, `--mode opencode` for ingest/enrich, `--opencode-binary/model/timeout` flags and `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_OPENCODE_*` env vars. Fallback chain extended to `codex â†’ claude â†’ opencode â†’ none`. 24-bug remediation including `--skip-embedding-on-failure` activation, `--llm-fallback` dead-flag fix, FTS desync in batch/enrich, Windows cross-compilation, embedding timeout hardcode, `list` pagination, `--yes` enforcement on destructive commands
- v1.0.91 spawn CWD isolation: `spawn_isolation_dir()` and `apply_cwd_isolation()` in `src/spawn/mod.rs` set `current_dir` to an ephemeral temp directory and `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` to the same dir for ALL 10 production spawn sites. LLM subprocesses no longer inherit `.mcp.json` from the caller's project â€” embedding works zero-config in any directory. `cleanup_spawn_dir()` removes the temp dir at process exit. BUG-17 fixed `entities.degree` inflation: `increment_degree()` replaced by `recalculate_degree()` after all relationships are inserted. BUG-15/16 fixed 8 JSON schemas with missing enum values and fields


## v1.0.93 Architecture (OpenRouter Embedding Backend)
- v1.0.93 adds optional OpenRouter REST API embedding backend via `--embedding-backend auto|openrouter|llm` global flag (ADR-0052, GAP-OR-INGEST)
- `EmbeddingBackendChoice` enum (`Auto`, `Openrouter`, `Llm`) is separate from `LlmBackendChoice` â€” embedding and LLM extraction are independent selections
- `--embedding-model MODEL` selects the OpenRouter model; NO default â€” user MUST specify when using `--embedding-backend openrouter`
- `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` env var or `--openrouter-api-key` flag provides the API key (handled via `secrecy::SecretString` with zeroize-on-drop, NEVER logged)
- Direct REST API via `reqwest+rustls-tls` to `POST https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/embeddings` â€” ~100-500ms vs 20-60s subprocess LLM cold-start
- MRL (Matryoshka Representation Learning) truncation to configured `--embedding-dim` (default 384)
- 10 verified models: `qwen/qwen3-embedding-4b`, `qwen/qwen3-embedding-8b`, `nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-vl-1b-v2:free`, `openai/text-embedding-3-small`, `openai/text-embedding-3-large`, `perplexity/pplx-embed-v1-0.6b`, `mistralai/mistral-embed-2312`, `baai/bge-m3`, `google/gemini-embedding-001`, `google/gemini-embedding-2`
- `EmbeddingBackendChoice` propagated to ALL 8 embedding commands: `remember`, `remember-batch`, `ingest`, `recall`, `edit`, `restore`, `hybrid-search`, `deep-research`
- `--enrich-after` flag on `ingest` triggers `enrich --operation memory-bindings` sequentially after all files are ingested
- BUG-OR-1 through BUG-OR-5: input_type per model, MRL detection, model validation, HTTP 200 malformed retry, dimension override


## Primary Documentation
### Canonical English sources for LLM ingestion
- [README](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/blob/main/README.md): full installation, command reference, integration table and FAQ
- [HOW_TO_USE](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/blob/main/docs/HOW_TO_USE.md): step-by-step walkthrough from install to first hybrid search in 60 seconds
- [COOKBOOK](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/blob/main/docs/COOKBOOK.md): 34+ recipes covering ingestion, retrieval, graph traversal, backup, audit, and the v1.0.76 LLM-only migration
- [AGENTS](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/blob/main/docs/AGENTS.md): persuasive guide for AI agent authors: economics, JSON contract, exit code routing
- [MIGRATION](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/blob/main/docs/MIGRATION.md): upgrade path v1.0.74/v1.0.75 â†’ v1.0.76 â†’ v1.1.0
- [INTEGRATIONS](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/blob/main/INTEGRATIONS.md): vendor-specific configuration for all 27 supported agents and IDEs
- [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): full release history with migration notes
- [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): pull request workflow and coding standards
- [SECURITY](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/blob/main/SECURITY.md): responsible disclosure policy and contact channel
- [CODE_OF_CONDUCT](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md): community standards


## Core Commands
### Subcommands grouped by lifecycle
- `init` initializes the SQLite database and validates that an LLM CLI (`claude`, `codex` or `opencode`) is reachable on `PATH`. No model download.
- `remember` saves a memory with name, type, description, body and optional entity graph; spawning an LLM subprocess per call for embedding generation
- `recall` performs pure-Rust cosine KNN similarity search over stored BLOB embeddings
- `hybrid-search` fuses FTS5 full-text and pure-Rust cosine KNN via Reciprocal Rank Fusion
- `deep-research` decomposes a query into up to 7 sub-queries, computes a separate embedding per sub-query (via the LLM subprocess), runs parallel vector+FTS hybrid search fused via RRF plus 3-hop graph traversal per sub-query, deduplicates and assembles directed evidence chains
- `read` `list` `forget` `rename` `edit` `history` `restore` manage the memory lifecycle
- `remember-batch` ingests multiple memories from a NDJSON stream in a single invocation
- `completions` generates shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, fish, elvish and powershell
- `link` `unlink` `related` manage typed entity relationships for multi-hop traversal
- `health` `stats` `migrate` `vacuum` `optimize` `sync-safe-copy` manage the database
- `migrate --rehash` rewrites recorded migration checksums to match the current file content (one-shot fix for v1.0.74 â†’ v1.0.76 V002 mismatch)
- `migrate --to-llm-only --drop-vec-tables` one-shot upgrade for v1.0.74/v1.0.75 databases: rehash + apply V013 + report vec-table state
- `backup` creates a consistent database backup using SQLite Online Backup API, safe with WAL mode
- `fts rebuild` `fts check` `fts stats` repair, verify, and inspect the FTS5 full-text index
- `memory-entities` lists all entities linked to a named memory
- `delete-entity` removes an entity and optionally cascades to its relationships
- `rename-entity` renames a graph entity preserving all FK-based relationships and re-embeds for vector search
- `reclassify` changes the `entity_type` of one entity or a whole category via `--batch`
- `merge-entities` merges duplicate entity nodes into a single canonical node
- `prune-ner` removes NER-generated bindings from one entity (`--entity`) or all (`--all --yes`)
- `remember --dry-run` previews parsing and graph extraction without writing to the database or spawning the LLM
- `cleanup-orphans` `prune-relations` remove orphaned entities and weak or unused relations from the graph
- `purge` `namespace-detect` handle maintenance and namespace resolution
- `reclassify-relation` renames relationship types across the graph
- `normalize-entities` normalizes all entity names in the namespace to lowercase kebab-case ASCII
- `enrich` LLM-augmented graph quality pipeline via `--mode claude-code` or `--mode codex`


## Environment Variables
### Runtime configuration surface
- `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_DB_PATH` explicit override for the SQLite database path
- Default behavior creates or opens `graphrag.sqlite` in the current working directory
- `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_CACHE_DIR` directory for cache and lock files (model cache only on builds â‰¤ v1.0.75)
- `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_EMBEDDING_DIM` embedding dimensionality override (v1.0.79, range [8, 4096], default 64)
- `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_CLAUDE_EMBED_MODEL` model override for `claude -p` embedding calls (v1.0.79)
- `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_EMBED_TIMEOUT_SECS` timeout per LLM embedding subprocess call (v1.0.79, default 300)
- `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_LOG_LEVEL` tracing filter level with values `error` through `trace`
- `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_LANG` CLI output language with values `en` or `pt`
- `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_NAMESPACE` namespace override respected by all commands (fixed in v1.0.51)
- `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` API key for OpenRouter embedding backend (v1.0.93); handled via `secrecy::SecretString`
- `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_EMBEDDING_BACKEND` persistent embedding backend selection (`auto`, `openrouter`, `llm`)
- `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_EMBEDDING_MODEL` persistent embedding model for OpenRouter backend


## Graph Input
### Minimal payload contract for `remember`
- `--entities-file` expects a JSON array of entity objects
- Each entity must include `name` plus `entity_type` or alias `type`
- Valid `entity_type` values: `project`, `tool`, `person`, `file`, `concept`, `incident`, `decision`, `memory`, `dashboard`, `issue_tracker`, `organization`, `location`, `date`
- `--relationships-file` expects a JSON array of relationship objects
- Each relationship must include `source`/`from`, `target`/`to`, `relation`, and `strength`
- `strength` must be a float in `[0.0, 1.0]` and maps to `weight` in graph outputs
- File payload relations use underscore labels like `applies_to`, `depends_on`, and `tracked_in`; dashed aliases are normalized before storage
- CLI flags on `link` and `unlink` use dashed labels like `applies-to`, `depends-on`, and `tracked-in`


## Automatic Extraction
### URL-regex extraction (GLiNER removed in v1.0.79)
- Pass `--enable-ner` or set `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_ENABLE_NER=1` to activate on `remember` and `ingest`
- Since v1.0.79 this runs URL-regex extraction ONLY â€” the GLiNER zero-shot pipeline was removed with the `ner-legacy` feature
- `--gliner-variant`, `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_GLINER_MODEL` and `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_GLINER_THRESHOLD` are accepted for compatibility but have NO effect
- Response field `extraction_method`: `url-regex`, `regex-only`, or `none:extraction-failed`
- For LLM-curated extraction use `ingest --mode claude-code` or `ingest --mode codex`; for exact control use `--graph-stdin`
- `--skip-extraction` is deprecated since v1.0.45 and has no effect
- `--max-rss-mb <MiB>` on `remember` and `ingest` aborts embedding when process RSS exceeds the threshold (default 8192 MiB)

## Ingest Modes (v1.0.62)
### Three extraction modes for bulk ingestion
- `--mode none` (default): body-only ingestion, no entity/relationship extraction
- `--mode gliner`: accepted for compatibility, but URL-regex extraction only since v1.0.79 (GLiNER/ONNX removed)
- `--mode claude-code`: LLM-curated extraction via locally installed Claude Code CLI binary
- Claude Code mode spawns `claude -p` headless with `--json-schema` for structured entity/relationship output
- Requires Claude Code >= 2.1.0 installed on the user's machine with active Pro/Max subscription
- Resumable via `--resume` flag with SQLite queue DB tracking per-file progress
- Per-file subprocess timeout via `--claude-timeout <S>` (default 300s); kills hung `claude -p` processes
- Budget control via `--max-cost-usd <N>` to limit cumulative API spend
- Rate limit handling with automatic exponential backoff (60s â†’ 120s â†’ 300s â†’ 900s)
- Output is NDJSON: per-file events with `entities`, `rels`, `cost_usd` fields, followed by summary
- `--mode codex` spawns `codex exec --json` per file for LLM-curated extraction via OpenAI Codex CLI
- Requires Codex CLI installed locally; uses `--output-schema` for structured JSON output
- Codex-specific flags: `--codex-binary <PATH>`, `--codex-model <MODEL>`, `--codex-timeout <S>` (default 300)
- Environment variable `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_CODEX_BINARY` overrides PATH lookup
- Token usage tracking via `input_tokens` and `output_tokens` fields (cost_usd unavailable from Codex CLI)
- Full embedding pipeline applied to Codex-ingested memories for recall and hybrid-search

Authentication: OAuth works out of the box for both modes â€” no API key needed.
--mode claude-code reads OAuth from ~/.claude/.credentials.json (Claude Pro/Max/Team).
--mode codex reads device auth from codex auth login (OpenAI).
API keys (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY) ABORT the spawn with AppError::Validation. OAuth is the ONLY accepted credential flow.


## Exit Codes
### Deterministic status for pipeline routing
- `0` success: continue the agent loop
- `1` validation or runtime failure: log and surface to operator
- `2` invalid CLI argument: fix usage then retry
- `9` duplicate memory detected: skip or use `--force-merge`
- `3` optimistic update conflict: re-read `updated_at` and retry
- `4` memory or entity not found: handle missing resource gracefully
- `5` namespace could not be resolved: pass `--namespace` explicitly
- `6` payload exceeded configured limits: split body into smaller chunks
- `10` SQLite database error: run `health` to inspect integrity
- `11` embedding generation failed: check LLM CLI availability and retry
- `13` batch partial failure: honor backoff and retry later
- `14` filesystem I/O error: cache directory not writable, nonexistent ingest target directory
- `15` database busy after retries: wait and retry
- `16` pre-flight validation failed (v1.0.87+): fix MCP config or binary path, see envelope for variant
- `19` SHUTDOWN: graceful shutdown interrupted work, partial state discarded, RETRY REQUIRED
- `20` internal or JSON serialization error
- `75` EX_TEMPFAIL: all concurrency slots busy OR job singleton locked, retry with backoff
- `77` available RAM below minimum required for LLM subprocess


## What Changed in v1.0.79
### G42: fast, parallel, robust LLM embedding pipeline
- Configurable embedding dimensionality, default 384 (`--embedding-dim`; precedence flag > `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_EMBEDDING_DIM` > `schema_meta.dim` > 384; existing 384-dim databases keep working unchanged; ZERO schema change)
- Batched LLM calls: calibration bases of 8 chunks / 25 entity names at dim 64, adapted as clamp(baseĂ—64/dim, 1, base) per call (`{items:[{i,v}]}` schema, G44) â€” 39 spawns collapse into 4-5
- Bounded parallelism (`Semaphore` + `JoinSet`): new `--llm-parallelism` on `remember` (default 4), `ingest` (default 2), `edit`; permits clamp [1, 32]
- `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_CLAUDE_EMBED_MODEL` and `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_EMBED_TIMEOUT_SECS` (default 300s) env overrides; `kill_on_drop` on every LLM subprocess
- Empty `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` by default on the embedding path (~40-50s â†’ ~10-15s per call)
- Panic-free signal handler: second signal exits 130 with zero I/O; SIGPIPE exits 141
- Canonical re-embed: `enrich --operation re-embed --limit N --resume` plus `edit --force-reembed`
- `validate_dim` errors on divergent vectors instead of silently truncating/padding
- REMOVED: daemon infrastructure; `embedding-legacy`/`ner-legacy`/`full` features and fastembed/ort/ndarray/tokenizers/hf-hub optional deps

## What Changed in v1.0.68
### Critical fixes (G28 + G29)
- v1.0.68 is the first release since v1.0.65 that compiles on Windows via `cargo install`.  v1.0.66 and v1.0.67 broke with `error[E0308]` in `src/terminal.rs:29` because `HANDLE` in `windows-sys >= 0.59` is `*mut c_void` (was `isize` in 0.48/0.52).  Fix: `!handle.is_null() && handle != INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE` plus `windows-sys` pinned to `=0.59.0` exact, plus CI job `windows-build-check`.
- `enrich`, `ingest --mode claude-code`, and `ingest --mode codex` now acquire a per-namespace singleton via `lock::acquire_job_singleton(job_type, namespace, wait_seconds)`.  A second concurrent invocation on the same database returns `AppError::JobSingletonLocked { job_type, namespace }` (exit 75, retryable) instead of stacking 4 Ă— N workers Ă— 10 MCP servers.
- `claude_runner::build_claude_command` now respects `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_CLAUDE_EMPTY_CONFIG_DIR` (opt-in).  When set to an empty directory, the subprocess is spawned with `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=<that dir>`, suppressing user-scoped MCP servers.  This is the only mechanism Claude Code actually honours â€” `--strict-mcp-config` and `--mcp-config '{}'` are silently ignored per [anthropics/claude-code#10787].
- `enrich` emits a `tracing::warn!` when `--llm-parallelism > 4`, recommending the combination with `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` override.
- `retry::CircuitBreaker` helper added with `AttemptOutcome::{Success, Transient, HardFailure}`.  Rate-limited and timeout errors are explicitly excluded from the failure count.
- 3 pre-existing test failures in `src/commands/{history,list,read}.rs` fixed (timezone-agnostic assertions).

## Optional References
### Supplementary materials for deeper context
- [CLAUDE guidance](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/blob/main/docs/CLAUDE.md): Claude Code specific invocation patterns and session memory strategies
- [SKILL definitions](https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag/tree/main/skill/sqlite-graphrag-en): pre-built slash-command skills for Claude Code harness
- [crates.io package](https://crates.io/crates/sqlite-graphrag): published binary with semver and MSRV metadata
- [docs.rs API reference](https://docs.rs/sqlite-graphrag): rustdoc for library consumers


## Stable Facts
### Identity and version metadata
- Package name `sqlite-graphrag` published on crates.io under MIT OR Apache-2.0
- Current version 1.0.99. v1.0.99 = GAP-SG-67 removed destructive degree-cap pruning + `--max-entity-degree` flag from remember/link (BREAKING, clap exit 2; writes now purely additive, never delete edges); GAP-SG-68 aligned `graph entities --sort-by degree` doc to ascending behaviour (`--order desc` for most-connected-first); GAP-SG-69 `enrich body-enrich --until-empty` converges (scan skips preservation-vetoed bodies); no migration, schema v15. v1.0.97 = enrich dead-letter recovery + write ergonomics: `--requeue-dead` (deadâ†’pending), `--list-dead` (lists dead with `error_class`/`message`), `--ignore-backoff` (bypass `next_retry_at`), `--prune-dead-orphans` (read-only inspector, deletes orphan dead memory entries whose key is gone from main db, GAP-SG-66, ADR-0058); `--status`/`--list-dead`/`--requeue-dead`/`--prune-dead-orphans` need no `--operation`/`--mode`; new `augment-bindings` operation (requires `--names`) and `body-extract --body-extract-graph-only`; `--max-attempts` default 8; `--openrouter-timeout` default 600s; `remember --graph-file`/`--strict-name`/`--replace-graph`; `ingest --force-merge` + `body_hash` dedup + native auto-split; `read --format raw`; `unlink --memory/--entity`; `embedding status` `coverage` object; `stats` top-level `total_memories`; `--db` after subcommand, `SQLITE_GRAPHRAG_DB_PATH` canonical (GAP-20, SG-32). v1.0.96 = enrich dead-letter + bounded REST fan-out (ADR-0055): `enrich --until-empty` internal scan->drain loop, dead-letter queue (`error_class`/`next_retry_at`/terminal `dead`) converges the backlog (GAP-ENRICH-BACKLOG-CONVERGE), new `--max-runtime`/`--max-attempts`/`--status`/`--rest-concurrency` flags, bounded `tokio::task::JoinSet` OpenRouter REST embedding fan-out (GAP-OPENROUTER-REST-CONCURRENCY). v1.0.95 = OpenRouter chat enrich (ADR-0054, GAP-OR-ENRICH): `enrich --mode openrouter` routes the judge to OpenRouter `/chat/completions` REST with no local CLI. v1.0.94 = four-gap remediation (ADR-0053): default embedding dim 384, enrich --mode required (exit 2 if omitted), embedding timeout 300s, entity embedding honours --embedding-backend/--llm-backend. v1.0.93 = OpenRouter REST API embedding backend (ADR-0052, GAP-OR-INGEST), `--embedding-backend auto|openrouter|llm`, `--enrich-after` for ingest, BUG-OR-1 through BUG-OR-5 fixes, `EmbeddingBackendChoice` propagated to all 8 embedding commands. v1.0.91 = spawn CWD isolation (GAP-SPAWN-001), degree recalculation fix (BUG-17). v1.0.90 = OpenCode backend. v1.0.89 = schema drift. v1.0.87 = pre-flight validation. v1.0.79 = LLM-only foundation. MSRV Rust 1.88. Binary 14.6 MiB.
- Repository `https://github.com/daniloaguiarbr/sqlite-graphrag` with CI on push and tag
- Embedding model: delegated to OpenRouter REST API (v1.0.93, ~100-500ms) OR headless `claude code` / `codex` / `opencode` subprocess (OAuth, ~20-60s) per call; no in-process model
- Storage layer: `rusqlite` with bundled SQLite and FTS5 module. `sqlite-vec` extension REMOVED in v1.0.76; vector similarity is pure-Rust cosine over BLOB embeddings
- Up to four concurrent instances supported via counting semaphore with `fs4` advisory locks
